description: §21.3 — importing `{ missing }` from a sibling m.scrml that exports only `present()` fires E-IMPORT-004 (imported name not found in the target's exports). m.scrml is present via the `files` convention, so E-IMPORT-006 does NOT fire — this isolates the name-resolution failure.
rationale: §21.3: an import binds names against the target module's export set. m.scrml exports `present`, not `missing`, so `import { missing }` is rejected E-IMPORT-004 (add `export missing` or import an available name).
